cards.front: What are the two keywords used to specify the type of an object in C++?
What is the purpose of the 'const' keyword in C++?
cards.back: To declare a variable that cannot be changed once it is initialized

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'export' keyword in C++?
cards.back: To declare a variable that can be accessed from other files

cards.front: What is the difference between 'const' and 'export' in C++?
cards.back: const refers to a variable that cannot be changed, while export refers to a variable that can be accessed from other files

cards.front: Can a variable be both 'const' and 'export' in C++?
cards.back: No, a variable can only be one or the other
